oracle.invocation: llama-server -m <apex.gguf> -ngl 99; POST /completion {prompt, n_predict:16, temperature:0, top_k:1, cache_prompt:false, return_tokens:true, n_probs:5}; prompt ids via POST /tokenize
oracle.note: Greedy continuations of DECISIVE prompts from the SAME GGUF file. The oracle must load the SAME weights: the APEX GGUFs are k-quant re-quantizations of the 35B base weights, so the safetensors-NVFP4 goldens are NOT the reference (APEX-Balanced legitimately diverges from the NVFP4 continuation at token 7 in the oracle itself). Both APEX files produce IDENTICAL continuations for these two prompts, and the oracle's per-step top-2 logprob margins are healthy (eiffel min 0.15 [Balanced pos 5] / 0.28 [Compact pos 6]; count min 1.91), so greedy agreement is well-defined across engine numerics (ours: dequant->bf16 + bf16 GEMMs, mirroring vLLM's GGUF recipe; oracle: native quantized ggml kernels).
oracle.m0_prompt_tie_disclosure: The M0-exit prompt ('The capital of France is Paris, and the') is NOT gated for GGUF: the oracle's own top-2 at the first generated token are a near-tie (' capital' -1.8802 vs ' official' -1.9200, margin 0.040), and our engine legitimately takes the other branch. Loader correctness was verified weight-level instead: every GGUF tensor family cross-checked against the safetensors checkpoint ground truth (norm +1 inversions, ssm_a/dt_bias/conv1d V-head reorders EXACT to 0.0; quantized families corr>=0.9927 with the documented reorder/transpose transforms).

prompts.eiffel: The Eiffel Tower is located in the city of

args.note: both files share identical oracle continuations for these prompts
